memoryArtifacts.headline:
Thomas Clarke, Sr.
(March 1568 – May 1638)
memoryArtifacts.description:
Thomas Clarke was born in Banham, Norfolk, England in March of 1613. 

He was baptized there on March 7, the son of Rowland and Margaret Micklewood Clark. 

On October 17, 1602, he wed Mary Canne at B …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Clarke Sr.於1567年3月出生在英國諾福爾可班納姆時，他的父親Rowland Clarke42歲，母親Margaret Martha Micklewood36歲。他於1602年10月17日與Mary Canne在英國諾福爾可班納姆結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1638年5月10日在他的家鄉享年71歲，埋葬在英國諾福爾可班納姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas Clarke Sr.于1567年3月出生在英国諾福爾可班納姆时，他的父亲Rowland Clarke42岁，母亲Margaret Martha Micklewood36岁。他于1602年10月17日与Mary Canne在英国諾福爾可班納姆结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1638年5月10日在他的家乡享年71岁，埋葬在英国諾福爾可班納姆。

nameMeanings.name: Clarke
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Clark  .

History: This name was brought independently to New England by many bearers from the 17th century onward. Nicholas Clarke was one of the founders of Hartford, CT (coming from Cambridge, MA with Thomas Hooker) in 1635.

variants.name: Clark
meanings: English: from Middle English clerk, clark ‘clerk, cleric, writer’ (Old French clerc; see Clerc ). The original sense was ‘man in a religious order, cleric, clergyman’. As all writing and secretarial work in medieval Christian Europe was normally done by members of the clergy, the term clerk came to mean ‘scholar, secretary, recorder, or penman’ as well as ‘cleric’. As a surname, it was particularly common for one who had taken only minor holy orders. In medieval Christian Europe, clergy in minor orders were permitted to marry and so found families; thus the surname could become established.
Irish (Westmeath, Mayo): in Ireland the English surname was frequently adopted, partly by translation for Ó Cléirigh; see Cleary .
Americanized form of Dutch   De Klerk   or Flemish   De Clerck   or of variants of these names, and possibly also of French   Clerc  . Compare   Clerk   2 and   De Clark  .

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Clarke Sr. was born in March 1567, in Banham, Norfolk, England, his father, Rowland Clarke, was 42 and his mother, Margaret Martha Micklewood, was 36. He married Mary Canne on 17 October 1602, in Banham, Norfolk, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 10 May 1638, in his hometown, at the age of 71, and was buried in Banham, Norfolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Clarke Sr. est né en mars 1567, à Banham, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Rowland Clarke, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Margaret Martha Micklewood, avait 36 ans. Il a épousé Mary Canne le 17 octobre 1602, à Banham, Norfolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 10 mai 1638, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 71 ans, et a été enterré à Banham, Norfolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas Clarke Sr. im März 1567 in Banham, Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Rowland Clarke 42 und seine Mutter Margaret Martha Micklewood 36. Er hat am 17. Oktober 1602 in Banham, Norfolk, England Mary Canne geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 10. Mai 1638 im Alter von 71 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Banham, Norfolk, England begraben.

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Clarke Sr.(이)가 1567년 3월 영국 노르폴크 반함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Rowland Clarke(은)는 42세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Martha Micklewood(은)는 36세였다. 그는 1602년 10월 17일 영국 노르폴크 반함에서 Mary Canne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1638년 5월 10일 그의 고향에서 71세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르폴크 반함에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Mary Canne em 17 de outubro de 1602, em Banham, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 10 de maio de 1638, em sua cidade natal, com 71 anos, e foi sepultado em Banham, Norfolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Clarke Sr. родился марта 1567 г. в местности банам, норфолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Rowland Clarke было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret Martha Micklewood, 36. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Canne 17 октября 1602 г. в местности банам, норфолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер 10 мая 1638 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 71 год и был похоронен в местности банам, норфолк, Англия.

Se casó con Mary Canne el 17 de octubre de 1602, en Banham, Norfolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 10 de mayo de 1638, en su pueblo, a la edad de 71 años, y fue sepultado en Banham, Norfolk, Inglaterra.
